# YourGP@Denman — Full Site Content

> General practice in Denman Prospect, Canberra — Genuine Care, Clinical Excellence.

---

## Home

A very warm welcome to YourGP@Denman.

Our practice vision is 'Genuine Care, Clinical Excellence'.

The best medicine is provided in the context of a long-term relationship with your GP. This relationship allows your GP to grow in understanding of who you are, your medical history and what is important to you. In this setting your GP can best tailor treatment to you and your circumstances, caring for you as a whole person. You can grow in trust of your GP, in their knowledge and genuine care for you and your family. Many GPs have established relationships with patients that cover decades. Some have the privilege of caring for four generations of the same family. These are relationships your GP would like to build with you.

Your GP provides clinical excellence through:
- Staying up to date with advances in medicine
- Fully computerised records and other technologies to support comprehensive care
- Continuing practice quality improvements
- Teaching the next generation of doctors by participating in the training of medical students and new GPs

Most importantly, your GP wishes to take the time to listen and explain. Your GP aims for people to leave with questions answered and a better understanding of their own health.

YourGP@Denman is partnered with YourGP@Crace and YourGP@Lyneham.

### Opening Hours

**Denman**: Monday to Friday 8:00am–6:00pm

### Locations

- YourGP@Denman — Level 1, 1B Felstead Vista, Denman Prospect ACT 2611
- YourGP@Crace — 1/5 Baratta Street, Crace ACT 2911
- YourGP@Lyneham — 62 Brigalow St, Lyneham ACT 2602

Phone: (02) 6109 0000

---

## Doctors

### Dr Elizabeth Muller
**New patients welcome**
Previously practising as a GP in Wanniassa, ACT, Elizabeth has developed experience across a broad range of general practice services — including children's health, preventive care, chronic disease monitoring and women's health. Elizabeth studied medicine at Australian National University (ANU), graduating with distinction in 2016. Following graduation, she completed her internship and residency in Canberra and Goulburn. She values compassionate care and continuous learning.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday, Tuesday, Friday

### Dr Michelle de Jesus
**New patients welcome**
Dr De Jesus graduated from the University of Western Australia in 2012. She has spent the last 12 years in hospital medicine gaining experience across a wide range of medical and surgical specialties at Royal Perth Hospital as well as at The Canberra Hospital and Calvary Bruce Public Hospital as a medical registrar. She enjoys all areas of medicine and has special interests in General Medicine and chronic disease management as well as Geriatric medicine.
**Availability**: Denman — Tuesday, Thursday

### Dr Adam Wiley
**New patients welcome**
Dr Adam is a Canberra local but undertook most of his medical degree rurally in Tamworth and Armidale. Returning to the ACT he has spent extra years rotating through various specialties of medicine such as obstetrics, intensive care and cardiology. With a passion for holistic health care, education and all areas of general practice he is actively welcoming new patients. He has completed a certificate in wilderness medicine.
**Availability**: Denman

### Dr Elliot Missen
**New patients welcome**
Elliott completed his medical degree from Monash University in 2019 and began training as a GP registrar in February 2024. He has gained substantial experience in various medical and surgical specialties across metropolitan and rural centres. He believes in a holistic approach to patient care and is passionate about all aspects of General Practice. His work in several surgical specialties, including paediatrics and orthopaedics, has given him a wealth of knowledge and expertise.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday

### Dr Tracy Fox
**New patients welcome** (for pregnancy, babies and lactation)
Dr Tracy Fox's main area of interest in general practice is working with kids and their families. Tracy worked at Westmead Children's Hospital before moving into general practice. She completed her Diploma of Child Health in 2010 and regularly keeps updated with Women and Child Health conferences. She has an interest in postnatal checks, childhood development checks, antenatal breast-feeding advice and lactation medicine. Tracy completed her medical degree with honours from the University of Sydney in 2007.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday, Wednesday

### Dr Maria Iannelli
**New patients welcome**
Maria enjoys all aspects of general practice but has a particular interest in paediatrics and adolescent health, women's health, preventive health care, and lactation medicine. Maria is a born and raised Canberran, although it was at the University of Melbourne that she obtained her Doctor of Medicine. She returned to Canberra to complete her training, specifically in General Practice due to her passion for quality care and patient advocacy.
**Availability**: Denman — Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday

### Dr Rachel Liang — Skin Cancer
**Accepting new patients for skin checks**
Dr Rachel Liang is a highly experienced specialised skin cancer GP. She is passionate about skin cancer diagnosis and surgery and has been working solely in this field for over a decade. She has many years of surgical experience and has undertaken extensive surgical training from a leading plastic surgeon. Dr Rachel Liang completed her medical degree at the University of Otago in 2006. She obtained certification in Skin Cancer Medicine and Surgery with the University of Queensland in 2013. She is a fellow of the Royal Australian College of General Practitioners and is completing her Master of Medicine Degree in Skin Cancer with the University of Queensland.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday

### Dr Chi So — Skin Cancer
**Accepting new patients for skin checks**
Dr Chi So specialises in skin cancer diagnosis and management. He is able to perform full body skin checks, mole and spot checks, dermoscopy, skin cancer surveillance, skin cancer excisions, biopsies, and minor procedures. He completed the requirements for a Fellowship of the Royal College of General Practitioners in 2017. He went on to complete a Master in Medicine (Skin Cancer) and a Master of Public Health. He has published research papers in international journals exploring the field of skin cancer management in Australian primary care.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday

### Dr Kathleen O'Brien
**Not accepting new patients**
Dr Kathleen O'Brien grew up in Canberra and is a graduate of the ANU Medical School. She holds a Diploma in Child Health (Sydney Children's Hospital Network/University of Sydney). She has interests in paediatrics, women's health, and preventive health.
**Availability**: Denman — Tuesday, Thursday

### Dr Somyata Jhorar
**Currently on maternity leave**
Somyata enjoys all areas of general practice however has a special interest in women's health, adolescent health and disease prevention. She completed her medical degree at the University of Queensland and holds a Diploma in Women's health.
**Availability**: Denman — Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday (when available)

### Dr James Manley
**Not accepting new patients**
Dr James Manley is a Canberra local and graduate of the ANU. His interests include pain management, paediatrics and chronic disease management. He is passionate about ensuring patients have continuity of care across the lifespan and enjoys seeing family units.
**Availability**: Denman — Wednesday, Thursday

### Prof Stephen Martin
**Not accepting new patients**
Prof Stephen Martin loves helping people of all ages with their health. He has a special interest in men's, paediatric and adolescent health. Originally from New Zealand, he trained as a doctor in Canberra, graduating in 2013 and has also completed a Diploma in Child Health. He has a passion for medical education and is the Professor of General Practice at The Australian National University.
**Availability**: Denman — Thursday

### Dr Anna Colwell — Travel Medicine
**New patients for travel consultations only**
Anna completed Medicine at the University of Tasmania. She has had further training and experience in public health, infectious diseases and travel medicine, including obtaining a Masters of Public Health and Tropical Medicine, and Fellowship with the Australasian College of Tropical Medicine. Anna has worked for the Commonwealth Government in relation to immunisation, outbreaks, disaster planning and veteran's health and was a member of the ACT Health COVID Response Team. She has interests in lifestyle medicine, infectious diseases and travel health.
**Availability**: Denman — Wednesday

### Dr Mel Deery
**Not accepting new patients**
Mel enjoys all areas of general practice with special interests in paediatrics, women's health, pregnancy care and mental health. Mel is a practice owner and is passionate about developing YourGP to better fulfil the vision of 'genuine care, clinical excellence'. Mel is a graduate of the University of New South Wales 2000.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday, Friday

### Dr John Deery — Vasectomy
**Accepting new vasectomy patients only**
John provides pre-vasectomy appointments on Monday afternoons at Denman. He is passionate about providing amazing health care to patients. John graduated from the University of NSW in 2000.
**Availability**: Denman — Monday; Lyneham — Wednesday; Crace — Thursday, Friday

### Dr Penny Hall
**Not accepting new patients**
**Availability**: Denman — Monday

---

## Services

### Children's Health
Paediatrics is a special interest of the GPs. Kids love to visit. The GPs and nurses all enjoy seeing children.

### Childhood Immunisations
GPs and nurses are supportive of childhood vaccinations. They are safe and an effective way to protect your child. Immunisation records are uploaded electronically to the Australian Immunisation Register.

### Chronic Disease Management
GPs are highly experienced at helping patients with:
1. Cardiovascular disease including myocardial infarction (heart attack) and post-MI care; valvular heart disease; hypertension (high blood pressure); heart failure.
2. Type 2 Diabetes both new-onset and established.
3. Osteoarthritis.
4. Chronic Kidney disease Stages 1 through 4.
5. Autoimmune diseases including rheumatoid arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, and psoriatic arthritis.

### Driver's Medicals
Commercial driving medical assessments require a double length appointment. For non-commercial licences for chronic medical conditions book a standard appointment.

### Men's Health
Check-ups, erectile dysfunction, incontinence, vasectomy, mental health and STD screens.

### Mental Health
Depression, anxiety, postnatal depression, schizophrenia, grief, bipolar depression and PTSD. A mental health care plan (referral to a psychologist) requires a double appointment.

### Minor Procedures
Implanon insertion and removal, IUD insertion and removal, skin cancer removal, ingrown toenail repair, tongue tie, vasectomy. Not all procedures are done by all doctors — please call the practice.

### IUD Insertion
Dr Kyla Bremner, or Dr Ros Mackenzie at YourGP@Crace. This involves a visit to discuss IUD suitability and then a separate appointment for insertion.

### Musculoskeletal
Diagnosis and assessment of all types of joint and muscle sprains and strains.

### Tongue Tie
Dr John Deery only. Tongue tie is a common condition of newborn children. It will sometimes cause newborns to have a lot of difficulty with breastfeeding. He can provide prompt assessment and treatment of the condition, on the same day, when appropriate. Lip tie concerns are common and will rarely need surgical correction.

### Travel Vaccinations
Yellow fever vaccine is available at Denman with Dr Anna Colwell or at Crace. Please bring any previous immunisation records. For a list of likely travel requirements please look up http://wwwnc.cdc.gov/travel/.

### Skin Checks
Dr Chi So and Dr Rachael Liang are GPs with a special interest in skin cancer. Please do not wear make-up. Skin checks require a long appointment.

### Vasectomy
Initial consult with Dr John Deery at Denman (Monday) or Lyneham (Wednesday). The procedure is performed at Crace on Thursday and Friday mornings. See canberravasectomy.com.au for more information.

### Women's Health
Check-ups, pap smears, STI screens, breast check, contraceptive advice, implanon insertion and removal, IUD insertion, menopause, pregnancy planning, shared antenatal care and postnatal check-ups.

### Workers Compensation
Payment is required at the time of your appointment. Standard consultation charges apply. Your insurer will reimburse you.

### Allied Health

**Pathology**: 4Cyte Pathology is available on-site, drop in without an appointment. They bulk bill, except for pathology that does not have a Medicare rebate. Opening hours 8am to 2pm, Monday to Friday. Located behind reception.

**Nursing**: Immunisations, ear syringe, wound management, health assessments, ECG, iron infusions, diabetes clinics, health assessments and chronic disease care plans. Bookings are not available online — please call for appointments.

---

## New Patient

### Your First Visit at YourGP@Denman

Save time waiting in the practice by completing your new patient registration online via HotDoc.

Please arrive at least ten minutes early to your first appointment. This will give you sufficient time to fill out forms and for reception to create your new patient file.

### Billing and Appointments

Payment is required at time of consultation. The GPs do not bulk bill.

With effect from 25 Nov 2025:
- Standard appointment fees are $117 ($107 for pensioners) with a Medicare rebate of $43.90
- Longer appointments over 20 minutes are $213 ($193 for pensioners) with a Medicare rebate of $84.90
- Saturday standard appointments (at Crace) are $146 and long appointments $240

All Workers Compensation cases are required to pay at time of consultation.

GPs reserve some appointments daily for urgent bookings. You can secure an 'on the day' appointment by booking online from midnight or by calling from 8:00am.

If you wish to cancel your appointment please give as much notice as possible. Missed appointments will attract a $50 fee.

### After Hours

Practice opening hours are 8am to 6pm Monday to Friday. A Saturday morning Crace clinic opens from 9am to 1pm.

For attention outside these hours please call CALMS on 1300 422 567.

CALMS (Canberra Afterhours Locum Service) is staffed by experienced Canberra GPs. It is a not-for-profit organisation that has been operating since 1971.

CALMS is only open after hours:
- 6pm–8:30am Monday to Friday
- 24 hours on weekends and public holidays

CALMS clinics operate until 11pm at Calvary Hospital Bruce and The Canberra Hospital. After 11pm a doctor is available to visit at home if you need urgent attention.

---

## FAQ

### Making Appointments
Book online via HotDoc or call the practice. If your usual GP is not available, you are welcome to see another GP at Crace, Denman or Lyneham — they will have full access to your computer record. Book a long appointment if you think your visit will require longer than fifteen minutes. Missed appointments without notice will attract a $40 fee.

### Urgent Bookings
A proportion of appointments are reserved for urgent bookings each day. You can secure an 'on the day' appointment by booking online after midnight or by calling from 8am. When fully booked, you can request a fit-in appointment.

### Billing
Payment is required at the time of consultation. The GPs do not bulk bill. Standard appointment fees are $117 ($107 for pensioners) with a Medicare rebate of $43.90. Longer appointments over 20 minutes are $213 ($193 for pensioners) with a Medicare rebate of $84.90. Saturday standard appointments are $146 and long appointments $240. All Workers' Compensation/Third Party cases are required to pay at the consultation time. Missed appointments will attract a $50 fee.

### Parking
There is easy and plentiful parking right outside the practice, including disabled parking.

### Languages
English. A phone interpreter can be provided if necessary — please give a few days notice. For deaf patients, the National Relay Service is available.

### Confidentiality
Patient information is kept confidential. You may request the full privacy policy from reception.

### Medical Certificates
A medical certificate is a legal document. Certificates cannot be backdated. Certificates will not be issued unless the GP is consulted.

### Results
The majority of results are notified via SMS. Any urgent results will be notified via phone call. Results are available online to view through MyHealthRecord or MyDHR. ACT Pathology results are available to view immediately through MyDHR or the MyHealthRecord. Capital Pathology and Laverty Pathology results are available to view through your MyHealthRecord 7 days after the results have been processed.

### Contacting Your Doctor
Your GP can be contacted during business hours. Your GP will only take urgent calls at the time. Non-urgent calls will be taken as a message and your call will be returned.

### Repeat Prescriptions
Your GP requests that you attend for review for a repeat prescription. This is best practice medicine. At your doctor's discretion a repeat prescription may be done without an appointment in exceptional circumstances. An administration fee of $30 will apply. Please allow four working days.

### E-Mail
E-mail to reception is not secure. To send a document to your doctor, please send to noreply@yourgpcanberra.com.au. Clinical advice cannot be given via email.

### Reminders
Your GP values preventative health care. You will be notified via SMS of reminders when due.

### After Hours Care
- CALMS (Canberra Afterhours Locum Medical Service): 1300 422 567
- Healthdirect (24/7 nurse helpline): 1800 022 222

### Accreditation
Accredited with AGPAL (Australian General Practice Accreditation Limited).

### Teaching Practice
YourGP is a teaching practice. Third year medical students from the ANU sit in with GPs for six-week rotations. GPs also teach and provide supervision to GP registrars. A registrar is a fully qualified doctor who is undertaking further training in General Practice. Registrars have several years postgraduate experience in the hospital system.

### Suggestions or Complaints
Feedback is welcome via the contact form. If unresolved, complaints can be directed to the Community & Healthcare Complaints Commissioner, GPO Box 321, Canberra ACT 2601.

---

---

## Careers

We care deeply about the people we serve. We pursue excellence every day. We continually seek to improve. We want to be the best.

### For GPs
YourGP as your chosen service entity strives to provide the best administrative and nursing support to GPs to enable you to focus on providing the best care for your patients. GPs are sole traders who conduct their own business and see their own patients.

### For Nurses
Our nursing team are integral to the high level of care we provide to our patients and GPs. Roles are varied with a mixture of jobs throughout your day including a lot of face-to-face patient time and health coaching.

### For Admin
The GPs saw over 100,000 patients last year. A lot of support is required for the clinical team. Qualities needed include enjoying learning new things, being energised by helping others, and excellent data entry skills.

---

## Privacy Policy

YourGP maintains a comprehensive privacy policy covering collection, use, storage, and disclosure of personal health information. The full privacy policy is available at https://www.yourgpdenman.com.au/privacy-policy/
